description: Milestone 01 exact frame review: deliver a local-first review slice with indexed videos, backend-canonical exact frame retrieval, and frame-by-frame navigation.

userStories.title: Add Video model and persisted metadata for indexed videos
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a stored Video record with milestone-01 metadata so that the app can treat backend indexing as the source of truth for review.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a persisted `Video` model that stores the milestone-01 metadata needed for review: stable id, source path, display name, frame count, fps, width, height, and duration when available
Document the stored `Video` fields for milestone 01 in `docs/engineering/data-model.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement backend video indexing service
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a backend service to scan local videos and upsert indexed metadata so that the UI can browse available review targets without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a typed video indexing service that scans the configured local video source and upserts `Video` records
Indexed metadata is derived on the backend, not from browser playback state
Document the indexing flow in `docs/engineering/architecture.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add list and detail video API endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want the frontend to fetch indexed videos and video details so that I can pick a video and inspect its review metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `GET /api/videos` to return indexed videos with milestone-01 review metadata
Implement `GET /api/videos/{video_id}` to return one indexed video or a 404 when the id is unknown
Request and response shapes are parsed at the API boundary
Document both endpoints in `docs/engineering/api.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add exact frame retrieval endpoint
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the backend to return frame N by canonical frame index so that annotation review uses exact decoded frames instead of browser video time.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `GET /api/videos/{video_id}/frame/{frame_idx}` to return the backend-decoded exact frame for the requested indexed video
Reject negative or out-of-range frame indices with a clear client error response
A regression test verifies repeated requests for the same `video_id` and `frame_idx` return stable exact-frame content
Document the exact-frame endpoint and canonical frame-index rule in `docs/engineering/api.md` and `docs/engineering/architecture.md`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add typed frontend video review data module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a feature-scoped frontend data module for list, detail, and frame requests so that review UI stays typed and keeps backend frame index as the canonical state.
acceptanceCriteria: Add typed frontend client methods for the video list, video detail, and exact-frame endpoints
Parse backend payloads at the frontend boundary before they enter review state
Expose selected-video state and current frame-index state through a small feature-scoped module
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build video list and selection UI
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to browse indexed videos and select one so that I can open it in the review workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: Render a video list view backed by `GET /api/videos`
Selecting a video loads its detail state and marks it as the active review target
Loading and empty states render without breaking the selection flow
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Add playback pane and backend metadata panel
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to watch the source video and see its metadata so that I can compare playback context with exact-frame review without treating playback time as annotation truth.
acceptanceCriteria: The selected video view shows a playback pane for the source video
The selected video view shows backend metadata needed for milestone 01: display name, frame count, fps, resolution, and duration when available
Playback controls do not become the source of truth for annotation frame selection
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Add exact frame pane with jump-to-frame input
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to enter frame N and see the backend-decoded exact frame so that I can inspect the canonical annotation image directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Render an exact-frame pane separate from the playback pane
A frame-number input accepts frame N and requests `GET /api/videos/{video_id}/frame/{frame_idx}` for that canonical index
Re-entering the same frame N produces a stable exact-frame image across repeated requests
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser

userStories.title: Add previous and next exact-frame navigation
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to step to frame N-1 and N+1 from the current exact frame so that I can review neighboring frames without losing the canonical backend frame index.
acceptanceCriteria: Add previous and next actions that move the exact-frame pane by -1 and +1 from the current canonical frame index
Navigation clamps at the first and last indexed frame
The displayed frame number stays in sync with the exact-frame image after each step
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser
